title: echenais carlinoides
intro: Echenais carlinoides, also known as the St. John's Wort of the Andes, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of South America. It is a member of the Rubiaceae family and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Echenais carlinoides can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, colds, and skin conditions.
